name: Boeing Leadership (Dennis Muilenburg / Dave Calhoun)
said: Safety is our number one priority.
did: 346 people killed in two 737 MAX crashes (Lion Air 2018, Ethiopian Airlines 2019).
Internal documents show employees knew about MCAS design flaws.
Pressured FAA to reduce safety oversight — allowed self-certification.
Hid MCAS system information from pilots and airlines.
Deferred prosecution agreement for fraud — DOJ found Boeing conspired to defraud regulators.
facts: DOJ: Boeing 'conspired to defraud' FAA in 737 MAX certification.
Internal messages: 'This airplane is designed by clowns who in turn are supervised by monkeys.'
346 killed. Boeing knew about MCAS problems before second crash.
Whistleblowers retaliated against — John Barnett died during lawsuit testimony.
DOJ revoked deferred prosecution agreement 2024 — criminal charges possible.

name: Wells Fargo Leadership (John Stumpf / Tim Sloan)
did: 3.5 million fake accounts opened without customer consent.
Employees under extreme sales pressure fired for not meeting quotas.
Charged customers fees on accounts they didn't know existed.
CEO Stumpf initially blamed low-level employees.
Fined $3B+ across multiple regulatory actions.
facts: OCC/CFPB: 3.5 million unauthorized accounts.
DOJ/SEC: $3B settlement for 'staggering' fraud.
5,300+ employees fired for creating fake accounts.
Federal Reserve: unprecedented growth cap imposed on entire bank (2018, still in effect).
Stumpf clawed back $69M, barred from banking industry.

name: Volkswagen Leadership (Martin Winterkorn)
said: We are committed to clean diesel.
VW is an environmentally responsible company.
did: Installed defeat devices in 11 million vehicles to cheat emissions tests.
Cars emitted up to 40x the legal limit of nitrogen oxides in real driving.
Lied to regulators and customers for years.
CEO Winterkorn charged with fraud — German and US indictments.
Cost: $30B+ in fines, settlements, and buybacks.
facts: EPA: VW used defeat devices in 11 million diesel vehicles worldwide.
DOJ: VW pleaded guilty to conspiracy to defraud, wire fraud, Clean Air Act violations.
$30B+ total cost in fines and settlements.
Winterkorn indicted for fraud (Germany) and charged by DOJ (US).
Estimated 1,200 premature deaths in Europe from excess emissions (MIT study).

name: Meta / Facebook (Mark Zuckerberg)
did: Cambridge Analytica: 87 million users' data harvested without consent.
Internal research showed Instagram harms teen mental health — hid the findings.
Myanmar: UN found Facebook 'played a determining role' in Rohingya genocide.
Failed to remove hate speech that fueled Ethiopian civil war.
Fined $5B by FTC for privacy violations — largest in history.
facts: FTC: $5B fine for deceiving users about privacy (2019).
Frances Haugen whistleblower: leaked internal research on teen harm, political misinformation.
UN Fact-Finding Mission on Myanmar: Facebook's role was 'significant.'
Cambridge Analytica: 87M profiles harvested, used in political manipulation.
SEC: Zuckerberg personally approved data-sharing deals despite public privacy promises.
40+ state AG lawsuits alleging harm to children through addictive design.
Internal research: '32% of teen girls said Instagram made body image issues worse.'
sources: Frances Haugen Senate testimony + leaked documents

name: Nestlé Leadership
did: Baby formula marketing in developing countries — WHO documented deaths from contaminated water mixing.
Child labor in cocoa supply chain — documented for 20+ years, promises repeatedly broken.
Water extraction: pumped water from drought-stricken communities for bottled water profits.
2021: admitted 60% of product portfolio 'not healthy' (internal presentation leaked).
facts: WHO/UNICEF: aggressive formula marketing in developing countries linked to infant mortality.
US Dept of Labor: child labor in Nestlé cocoa supply chain (Ivory Coast, Ghana).
Nestlé promised to end child labor in cocoa by 2005. Then 2008. Then 2010. Still documented in 2023.
Internal document (leaked 2021, Financial Times): 60% of products fail 'recognized definition of health.'
Permit controversy: extracted water from California during historic drought.
Sued by 11 former child slaves in cocoa supply chain (Nestlé USA v. Doe, Supreme Court).
sources: WHO International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes
US Dept of Labor child labor reports
Financial Times — leaked internal health assessment
Supreme Court: Nestlé USA v. Doe
NORC University of Chicago — cocoa child labor surveys

name: Rabobank Leadership
said: We are a cooperative bank serving our members.
did: Pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy for LIBOR manipulation.
Paid $1.07 billion in fines — DOJ, FCA, CFTC, Dutch prosecutors.
Traders systematically rigged global interest rates for profit.
Failed to prevent money laundering — $369M fine from Dutch prosecutors (2024).
CEO Moerland resigned. Multiple traders convicted.
facts: DOJ: Rabobank pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy (Oct 2013) — $325M DOJ penalty.
Total LIBOR fines: $1.07B across US, UK, and Dutch authorities.
CFTC: 'Rabobank, through the acts of its traders, attempted to manipulate LIBOR.'
Dutch Public Prosecution Service: €774M settlement for money laundering failures (2024).
Multiple individual traders criminally charged and convicted.
Internal communications showed systematic rate manipulation requests.

name: Goldman Sachs (David Solomon / Lloyd Blankfein era)
did: 1MDB scandal: helped steal $4.5 billion from Malaysian people.
Paid $2.9B DOJ penalty — largest ever for a Wall Street firm for foreign bribery.
Goldman bankers personally received $1B+ in bribes.
Pleaded guilty (Malaysian subsidiary) to bribery and money laundering.
2008: sold mortgage securities to clients while secretly betting against them.
facts: DOJ: Goldman Sachs admitted to bribery conspiracy related to 1MDB ($2.9B penalty).
Malaysian subsidiary pleaded guilty.
Goldman banker Tim Leissner pleaded guilty to conspiracy to launder money.
1MDB funds used for: Wolf of Wall Street film production, luxury yachts, jewelry, real estate.
SEC: $550M settlement for misleading investors on mortgage securities (2010).
Blankfein: 'We're doing God's work' (2009, during financial crisis).
sources: Senate Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations — Goldman hearings

name: ExxonMobil Leadership (Rex Tillerson / Darren Woods era)
said: We support the Paris Agreement goals.
did: Internal scientists predicted climate change accurately in 1977. Company funded denial for 40 years.
Spent $30M+ per year on climate denial lobbying and think tanks.
Public messaging contradicted own internal science for decades.
Fought every major climate regulation while publicly claiming support.
facts: Harvard study (Science, 2023): Exxon scientists' predictions from 1977-2003 were 'ichingly accurate' on global warming.
Union of Concerned Scientists: Exxon funded climate denial organizations with $33M (1998-2014).
NY AG investigation: Exxon misled investors about climate risk costs.
Exxon's own internal documents showed they knew fossil fuels caused climate change since 1977.
Lobbied against Clean Air Act, Kyoto Protocol, Paris Agreement implementation.
sources: Science (2023) — Supran, Rahmstorf, Oreskes: Exxon climate predictions study
Union of Concerned Scientists — Exxon funding analysis

name: Johnson & Johnson Leadership
did: Knew baby powder contained asbestos for decades — internal documents.
Marketed opioids aggressively — Oklahoma: $465M judgment for fueling opioid crisis.
Used 'Texas Two-Step' bankruptcy to avoid paying cancer victims.
38,000+ talc lawsuits from cancer patients.
facts: Reuters investigation (2018): J&J knew for decades that baby powder contained asbestos.
Internal memos from 1971 onward showed asbestos contamination knowledge.
Oklahoma court: $465M judgment for role in opioid epidemic.
38,000+ lawsuits from women alleging talc caused ovarian cancer.
Created subsidiary (LTL Management) to file for bankruptcy and limit victim payouts — 'Texas Two-Step.'
Supreme Court rejected bankruptcy strategy (2023) — must face victims in court.
sources: Oklahoma Dist. Court judgment (State v. J&J)
